target.name: Dexterity

methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company, carries a current post-money valuation of $1.65B following $291M in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws from a comp universe of nine private robotics firms at Series B stage, with a median peer valuation of $500M and a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. One public comparable exists in the dataset.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a forward valuation of $3.1B based on the $1.6B last post-money. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables—including 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), and three others valued between $300M and $500M—yields a median-based indication of $500M. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous risk and scale profiles within early-stage robotics.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.6B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Dexterity's current valuation of $1.65B sits near the lower-median bound. Key uncertainties include the applicability of stage-progression assumptions given sector volatility and whether peer comparables adequately reflect Dexterity's specific technology maturity and customer traction. The five-peer cohort exhibits extreme variance, limiting confidence in peer-median inference.
